decision: Proceed from the 20-symbol stress pilot to a 100-150 presentation-asset batch.

basis.notable_finding: Semantic QA caught a real generator bug: west cardinal lost the repeated yellow band when colors were deduped. The bug was fixed and covered.

selection_rules: Prefer whole symbol families over isolated symbols.
Include every sibling needed for forced-choice discrimination.
Include conditional pairs for wrecks, obstructions, lights, and restricted/caution/anchorage areas.
Include at least 20 area patterns and line styles, not only point symbols.
Include symbols whose S-52 lookup depends on attributes such as CATCAM, COLOUR, COLPAT, BOYSHP, BCNSHP, CATWRK, CATOBS, WATLEV, LIGHTS, and VALSOU.
Every item must have an S-52 lookup/instruction, Chart No.1 reference target, invariants, sibling set, deliberate broken cases, and hard-pile reason codes.

risks_before_99_percent: Chart No.1 crop acquisition and naming must be automated or the catalog becomes manual bottleneck.
Current semantic QA is rule-based and must be extended per family before full S-52 coverage.
Line styles and area patterns need first-class generation and tiling checks.
The clean-IP counsel gate remains required before treating the generated pack as relicensable production artwork.
A human hard-pile review workflow is still needed for ambiguous or rare symbols.
definition_of_done_for_next_batch: 100-150 selected assets are cataloged with provenance and sibling sets.
All selected assets generate across two styles with CSS-var colors and valid anchors.
All selected assets render across day/dusk/night palettes.
Valid generated cases meet or exceed 98% acceptance, with all failures in hard-pile.
Every deliberate broken case rejects for an expected reason code.
No silent safety miss is observed.
A scale report recommends either bulk expansion, another corrective batch, or stop.
